mappings.rationaleAI: Hinduism addresses religious developments during the Delhi sultanate (A.1.e) and Vijayanagar cultural development (B.2.b).
Islam provides context for Muslim hegemony (A, A.1), Deccan sultanates (B.1), and Mughal Empire (C).
Islamic history covers the Delhi sultanate (A.1), Islamic culture (A.1.e), and Mughal administration (C).
Mongols connect to the Tughluq-era Mughal invasion (A.1.c) and Mughal origins (C.1).
Indian philosophy covers cultural developments during the sultanate (A.1.e) and Vijayanagar (B.2.b).
Modern India provides continuity from Mughal decline (C.4).
The Quran connects to Islamic religious developments during the sultanate (A.1.e).
Dynastic rule covers sultanate dynasties (A.1) and the full Mughal period (C, C.1-C.4).
Silk Road connects to Mughal Central Asian origins (C.1).
Global economic history connects to Mughal economic policies (C.3).
Pakistan's territory was central to the sultanate (A.1) and Mughal governance (C).
Crusades provide context for Islamic military expansion (A.1).
Islamic philosophy connects to sultanate cultural developments (A.1.e).
Southeast Asia provides regional context for the Deccan (B) and Ceylon (E).
